content:
💡 mxcheck | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Scanning and assessing the security and configuration of email servers.
✨ Key Features: • DNS record checks (A, MX, PTR, SPF, MTA-STS, DKIM, DMARC) • StartTLS and certificate validation • Open port scanning (25, 465, 587) • Blacklist status checking • Information leakage detection (server string, VRFY command)
📖 Summary: mxcheck is a Go-based scanner for e-mail servers, providing comprehensive checks on DNS records, security protocols, open ports, blacklist status, and potential vulnerabilities like open relays and SMTPSmuggling. It allows for customizable sender/receiver addresses and outputs reports in TSV format.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 frame | Svelte
🎯 Primary Use Case: A user-friendly desktop application for converting and transcoding video and audio files with advanced options and AI upscaling.
✨ Key Features: • Support for various media containers (mp4, mkv, webm, etc.) • Multiple video and audio encoders (H.264, H.265, VP9, AV1, AAC, Opus, etc.) • Hardware acceleration support (Apple, NVIDIA) • Bitrate control (CRF, Target Bitrate) • Metadata probing via ffprobe
📖 Summary: Frame is a high-performance media conversion utility that provides a native graphical interface for FFmpeg operations. It allows users to control transcoding parameters, leverage hardware acceleration, and even perform AI upscaling, all managed by a Rust backend and a Svelte frontend.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 terminalizer | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Recording terminal sessions to create animated GIFs or web player links for demonstrations, tutorials, or sharing command-line workflows.
✨ Key Features: • Record terminal sessions • Generate animated GIF images • Shareable web player links • Highly customizable recording and output • Cross-platform compatibility
📖 Summary: Terminalizer is a command-line tool that records terminal sessions and generates animated GIF files or shareable web player links. It offers extensive customization for recording parameters, output styles, and terminal themes, making it ideal for creating demonstrations and tutorials.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 gosuki | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a unified, private, and synchronized bookmark management solution across various browsers and devices without relying on cloud services or browser extensions.
✨ Key Features: • Extension-free bookmark management • Multi-browser and multi-profile support • Real-time synchronization (local or P2P) • Portable database • Cloudless and privacy-first design
📖 Summary: GoSuki is a privacy-focused, extension-free bookmark manager written in Go. It unifies bookmarks across multiple browsers and profiles, offering real-time local or P2P synchronization with a portable database and optional archiving capabilities.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 CodeMachine-CLI | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automating and standardizing complex, multi-step coding tasks by orchestrating AI agents into defined workflows.
✨ Key Features: • Orchestrates AI coding agents into workflows • Enables repeatable, long-running workflows • Supports multi-agent orchestration and communication • Allows for parallel execution of agents • Handles workflow persistence for long-running tasks
📖 Summary: CodeMachine-CLI is an open-source tool designed to orchestrate AI coding agents into repeatable, long-running workflows. It allows developers to define complex coding processes once and then execute them reliably, automating repetitive tasks and enabling multi-agent collaboration.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ alt-sendme | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Direct, secure, and unrestricted file and folder transfers between users without intermediaries or cloud storage.
✨ Key Features: • Send anywhere (local networks or continents) • Peer-to-peer direct transfer (no cloud) • End-to-end encryption (QUIC + TLS 1.3) • No accounts or personal info required • Transfer anything (any size, any format)
📖 Summary: AltSendme is a free, open-source, and privacy-focused tool for direct peer-to-peer file transfers. It enables users to send any file or folder of any size across the globe without relying on cloud storage, ensuring end-to-end encryption and no account requirements.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
